docstring:
/**
 * @brief libstdc++ `std::endl` manipulator (char specialization, .isra.0 clone).
 *
 * Inserts a newline character into @p ctx and flushes the stream, matching the
 * semantics of `std::endl<char, std::char_traits<char>>`. The implementation
 * resolves the stream's embedded `ios_base` subobject via the `vtable[-3]`
 * virtual-base offset, retrieves the cached `std::ctype<char>` facet from
 * offset `0xF0`, widens `'\n'` through that facet (or skips the dispatch on
 * the inline fast-path), and tail-calls the shared insert+flush helper
 * (`sub_11B0`).
 *
 * @param ctx       Pointer to the `std::basic_ostream<char>` to write to.
 *                  The first word must point at a valid ostream vtable so
 *                  that `vtable[-3]` yields the offset of the contained
 *                  `ios_base` subobject.
 * @param expected  Expected address of the `do_widen` vfunc thunk. When the
 *                  cached slot at `*(facet) + 0x30` already matches this
 *                  pointer, the explicit `do_widen('\n')` call is elided.
 *                  Passed in a caller-saved register by the outer template
 *                  wrapper that performs the widen() check.
 *
 * @return @p ctx, as returned by `sub_11B0` (the ostream_insert + flush
 *         helper). The function never returns a different stream pointer.
 *
 * @retval ctx Always; on the bad_cast path the call to `sub_12b0`
 *             (`__throw_bad_cast`) is `noreturn` so the trailing `return`
 *             is unreachable.
 *
 * @note Throws `std::bad_cast` (via `sub_12b0`) when the `ios_base`
 *       subobject has no cached `ctype<char>` facet at offset `0xF0`.
 * @note On the fast path (`facet+0x38 == NULL`) the widen step is skipped
 *       entirely because the cached newline is already `'\n'`.
 * @note This is the `.isra.0` interprocedural-SRA clone produced by GCC;
 *       the public symbol
 *       `_ZSt4endlIcSt11char_traitsIcEERSt13basic_ostreamIT_T0_ES6_`
 *       is the outer wrapper that supplies @p expected.
 */
assumptions: Branch polarity corrected per reviewer feedback: facet[+0x38] == 0 is the inline fast path; non-zero takes the sub_1290 / do_widen slow path.
sub_12b0 is treated as __throw_bad_cast (noreturn); the trailing return is unreachable but kept so the function type-checks.
sub_11B0 modelled as the shared '__ostream_insert + flush' tail helper returning std::ostream&; both epilogue blocks (L_195c and the fall-through) are identical tail-calls and collapsed.
'expected' (arg2) kept as an opaque incoming pointer compared against the ctype vtable's do_widen slot at *(*facet + 0x30); not renamed to a fabricated symbol.
vtable[-3] expressed as ((ptrdiff_t*)vptr)[-3] rather than the raw '*(ret-0x18)' byte arithmetic.
Stack push/pop and rsp adjustments dropped as prologue/epilogue artefacts.
The widened character on the fast path is materialised as a local 'nl' to document the movsx; the actual binary passes it via a caller-saved register to sub_11B0.
Used hypothetical typedefs (std_ostream, ctype_char, ctype_char_do_widen_fn) since C has no std::ostream; a real rebuild would include the libstdc++ headers.
None of the listed string literals (ELF_MAGIC_STR, LD_LINUX_X86_64_PATH, STR_GNU, UNKNOWN_BLOB_3B, FUNC_NAME_STRLEN) appear in this function and were not used.
divergences: [high] control_flow: Branch polarity inverted relative to the provided pseudocode. Original: `%zf = (t10 == 0); if (%zf) goto L_1973;` where L_1973 is the sub_1290/do_widen slow path, meaning zero -> slow path, non-zero -> fast inline movsx + sub_11b0. The rewrite treats zero as the fast path and non-zero as the slow path — the opposite.

[medium] added_call: Function signature gains a second parameter `expected` (arg2) compared against *(*facet + 0x30). The original pseudocode references arg2 only in the comparison but the std::endl ABI takes a single ostream& argument; introducing it as a formal parameter changes the visible interface and makes the comparison depend on an uncontrolled register value.

[low] dropped_side_effect: The `unknown(movsx)` side-effect on the fast path is represented only as a dead local; no actual sign-extension or argument is passed to sub_11B0. This is acknowledged in assumptions but means the generated C will not reproduce the register-passed char argument.

[low] other: Two distinct epilogue blocks (fall-through after movsx and L_195c) are collapsed into one call. They are byte-identical tail calls in the original, so this is a safe fold, but it hides that each path separately performs the pop/ret sequence.

[medium] dropped_call: On the fast path the original performs `unknown(movsx); 0x11b0();` — sub_11b0 is called with no ostream argument visible (the rbp=arg0 was set earlier and the epilogue pops rbp before the jump at the non-L_195c path). The rewrite passes `ctx` explicitly. This may or may not be correct depending on the actual ABI but differs from the literal pseudocode which shows `0x11b0()` without arguments on the fast path vs `0x11b0(rbp)` on L_195c.
